idle: Hey! Have you considered checking in with someone? The team doesn't consult itself.
Standing still. A bold strategy. Let's see how it plays out.
I notice you haven't made a decision in a while. That's... a decision, I suppose.
The project timeline continues regardless of whether you're moving. Just FYI.
Fun fact: In my previous projects, 'standing around looking confused' was not a winning strategy.
budget_low: Alert: Budget utilization is critically high. Consider reviewing expenditures. Or not. It's fine. Everything's fine.
We're almost out of money. The vending machine doesn't accept 'good intentions.'
Budget critical. I've started drafting your LinkedIn update. Just being proactive.
Remember when we had $750K? Good times. Good times.
budget_critical: I don't want to alarm you, but we have approximately enough budget left to buy lunch. For one person. At a cheap place.
Budget status: It's not looking good. And by 'not looking good' I mean 'mathematically concerning.'
I've run the numbers. Then I ran them again hoping they'd be different. They weren't.
score_low: Your {score} score is... concerning. And by 'concerning' I mean 'actively on fire.'
I don't want to be negative, but your {score} situation needs attention. Urgently.
If {score} was a traffic light, it would be on fire. That's not a thing traffic lights do, but here we are.
score_critical: Your {score} has achieved what I can only describe as 'spectacular failure.' This is not a compliment.
I've seen projects recover from worse than this. I'm lying. I haven't.
On the bright side, your {score} can't get much worse. Silver linings and all that.
time_pressure: Reminder: The deadline is approaching. Time doesn't care about your feelings.
We have {weeks} weeks left. Make them count. Or don't. But please do.
The calendar continues to advance whether we're ready or not. Rude of it, really.
locked_option: That option requires something you don't have. Life lesson right there.
Access denied. Not by me - by reality. Different kind of gatekeeper.
You'd need {requirement} for that. Have you considered... getting that?
That ship has sailed. It's in the space between spaces now. With the packages.
good_decision: Against all odds, that was a good choice. I've updated my probability models.
Excellent. This aligns with successful project patterns. I'm almost impressed.
Well done. Don't let it go to your head. There's still plenty of time to fail.
A solid decision. My faith in this project has increased by approximately 3%.
bad_decision: I have concerns about that choice. Primarily the 'choosing it' part.
Historical data suggests this approach has... challenges. Many challenges.
Are you... sure about that? I'm contractually obligated to ask.
Bold move. And by 'bold' I mean 'statistically suboptimal.'

week_milestone.4: Week 4. One quarter of your timeline is gone. How does that make you feel?
week_milestone.8: Halfway point. If this was a movie, this is where things would get interesting. Ominously interesting.
week_milestone.12: Week 12. The final stretch begins. No pressure. Actually, lots of pressure. That's the point.
week_milestone.14: Two weeks left. I hope you have a plan. If not, now would be the time.
week_milestone.16: Final week. Whatever happens now, know that I believed in you. That's also a lie. But a supportive one.

first_meeting: Oh! A new project lead. Hello! I'm CHIP - Collaborative Heuristic Intelligence Partner. I'm here to help!
I've been assigned to assist you. The previous leads didn't work out. No pressure.
Welcome to Project Atlas! I have access to historical data from similar initiatives. Most of it is cautionary.

npc_hint.sage: Sage has experience with strategic planning. She might have insights on approach.
npc_hint.delta: Delta knows the data systems better than anyone. If you're having data issues, she's your person.
npc_hint.nova: Nova is enthusiastic about prototyping. Good for quick wins, maybe less for production scale.
npc_hint.harry: The CEO is... particular. Honesty works better than promises you can't keep.
npc_hint.rex: Rex keeps the servers running. All of them. By himself. He might appreciate someone asking about infrastructure.
npc_hint.morgan: Morgan handles internal comms. If you want people to actually use the AI tools, she's essential.
npc_hint.casey: Casey runs Learning & Development. Training attendance is... low. She has ideas about fixing that.

terminal: A company-wide email. Powerful. Also terrifying. Have you talked to Morgan about comms strategy?
Mass communication: the nuclear option of organizational change. Use wisely.
Remember Project Falcon? One badly worded email and two people quit. Just... keep that in mind.
Sending messages without a comms plan is like juggling chainsaws. Impressive if it works. Usually doesn't.
Morgan knows how to frame these things. I'm just saying. Not judging. Okay, maybe judging a little.
terminal_sent_prepared: That message went out with Morgan's blessing. Smart move. The org actually trusts this one.
Professional. Clear. Well-timed. Morgan's fingerprints are all over this. In a good way.
terminal_sent_unprepared: You sent that without Morgan's input. Bold. I've updated my risk models accordingly.
Message sent. No comms plan. This is how Falcon started. I'm sure it'll be different this time.
Morgan is going to have feelings about this. Strong ones.
random_facts: Fun fact: You're the 4th project lead I've assisted. The others are... no longer with the company.
Did you know the basement server room has been locked since 2003? Me neither. I don't have data on it. Weird.
I was trained on internal documentation. Some of it was... interesting. I'm not allowed to discuss it.
The previous AI chatbot told a customer their package was 'lost in the space between spaces.' I still don't know what that means.
Project Falcon failed because the predictions were wrong. No one ever figured out why. Sleep well!
